reason: Verify: no global mutable state introduced; mu.Lock/Unlock around state writes; no Listen/Serve/Sleep inside mu; tests follow established patterns (table-driven where applicable).
Verify Stop signal uses context cancellation (not error-text matching); wsState.LastError text is user-facing message, not a wrapped error chain leaked through.
Verify log levels: each retry attempt = info, exhaustion = warning. Verify no sensitive info leaked (no env / token / home path in retry log).
Critical: verify NO new method was added to internal/app.App. The PRD explicitly forbids RetryProduceWS or any sibling; check that the stable Wails public surface is unchanged.
Verify ProducePage.vue change: n-alert pattern matches the existing queueState.last_error block (type, bordered prop, location ordering).
Verify any user-facing text only modified zh-CN.json; en-US.json untouched.
Verify frontend type-check passes (cd frontend && npm run build); imports use @/ alias; auto-generated wailsjs/ untouched.

Verify end-to-end: supervisor → wsState mutation → emit → produce_ws_state_changed event → useProducePage.ts subscribes (already does) → ProducePage.vue renders alert. No layer skipped, no field name drift between Go and TS.
Verify against acceptance criteria AC1–AC7 and Out of Scope: no port fallback, no config knob, no new Wails method, no HLAE process changes, no logging package import added to producews.
